result.summary: Linkly exposes one authenticated REST API for link management/analytics/webhooks, one OAuth-protected MCP server at mcp.linklyhq.com, and an npm-distributed CLI.
result.description: Linkly is a URL shortener and click-tracking platform for branded links and QR codes. It lets users create and manage short links, route traffic, track analytics, and automate link workflows.

linkly_api_key.label: Linkly API key
linkly_api_key.setup: Sign in to your [Linkly workspace](https://app.linklyhq.com), then open **Settings → API Keys** to create or copy your API key. Linkly's docs say the key is in your workspace settings, and their product pages direct developers to generate it under `Settings → API Keys`. Use the key as a Bearer token in `Authorization`, as HTTP Basic auth with the key as the username and an empty password, or as the `api_key` query parameter for testing.

linkly_mcp_oauth.label: Linkly MCP OAuth account authorization
linkly_mcp_oauth.setup: Connect your MCP client to `https://mcp.linklyhq.com` and complete the browser sign-in flow to Linkly. The MCP server publishes OAuth metadata at [authorization server metadata](https://mcp.linklyhq.com/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server) and [protected resource metadata](https://mcp.linklyhq.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource); release notes say the flow uses OAuth `authorization_code` with PKCE and dynamic client registration. If your MCP client supports remote OAuth MCP servers, it should discover these endpoints automatically.

variables.name: workspace_id
variables.description: Most endpoints are scoped to a workspace; use `GET /workspaces` to list accessible workspace IDs.
